response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer with broad full-stack and some systems experience, but he does not meet several hard requirements for this role. There is no evidence of native-level Japanese proficiency (languages listed are English, Romanian, German), no Linux kernel investigation or modification experience, and his C/UNIX exposure is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments a decade ago. The role's core domain — embedded LAN-switch firmware, MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3 networking — is absent from his background.
strengths: Broad, long-tenured software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded contexts
Systems-level work with low-latency, high-throughput services (DLang, async I/O) and C/C++ IDE components at National Instruments
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions, and knowledge sharing
concerns: No indication of native-level Japanese, a hard requirement for the role
No Linux kernel analysis/modification experience or embedded networking-security background (MACsec, 802.1X, switch chips)
C programming experience is dated and minimal relative to the 'strong C for UNIX' requirement; recent work is TypeScript/Node.js product development

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking/security or Linux kernel experience
Solid general engineering seniority and systems programming aptitude
